title: StringBuilder data should be preferred
description: "StringBuilder" data should be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellperformanceWhy is this an issue?StringBuilderinstances that never build astringclutter the code and worse are a drag on performance. Either they should be removed, or the missingToString()call should be added.Noncompliant code examplepublic void DoSomething(List<string> strings) { var sb = new StringBuilder(); // Noncompliant sb.Append("Got: "); foreach(var str in strings) { sb.Append(str).Append(", "); // ... } }Compliant solutionpublic void DoSomething(List<string> strings) { foreach(var str in strings) { // ..
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: StringBuilder instance is created but its data is never used (missing ToString() call)

never_trigger_on: Methods that call ToString() on the StringBuilder
Methods that return the StringBuilder result
logic.reasoning: StringBuilder instances that never build a string clutter the code and are a drag on performance. Either the StringBuilder should be removed, or the missing ToString() call should be added to use the built string.

should_trigger: var sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.Append("Got: "); foreach(var str in strings) { sb.Append(str); }
should_not_trigger: var sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.Append("Got: "); Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString());
var sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.Append("Got: "); return sb.ToString();
